title: C2 canonical process authorization anchor (PR-2) + verified-state provenance lock
intent: Supply the AUTHORIZED_CANONICAL_APPROVERS authorization anchor that PR-1 (#182) named as the PR-2 hard prerequisite — the seam deciding WHO may turn a governed process approval into verified_governed_process canonical authority. The anchor ships EMPTY and FAIL-CLOSED (allowed process/version/rule and roles declared, zero approver ids), so runtime behavior is unchanged: every governed approval still resolves to unverified_pending_governance until a separate repo-owner-ratified commit seeds the first approver id. Post-review hardening provenance-locks the verified state at BOTH the approval-record model validator and the reference boundary so a directly-constructed or rehydrated record cannot assert verified authority the anchor would not grant, routed through the same is_authorized_canonical_approver helper the factory uses to mint (mint-time and validation-time semantics cannot diverge). The oversized approval module is split — the governed-event derivation (derive_governed_approval_event_id) and CanonicalProcessApprovalError move to a sibling module and are re-exported — to hold the file-size ratchet green without raising the threshold.

diff_articulation.what_changed: Adds VERIFIED_GOVERNED_PROCESS to the allowed authentication states, the fail-closed AUTHORIZED_CANONICAL_APPROVERS anchor keyed on the ratified process/version/rule constants, and is_authorized_canonical_approver() (id AND role, system-actor-safe). Authentication is decided once in create_canonical_process_approval_record; the reference inherits it. Post-review hardening adds a provenance re-check of the verified state in the approval-record model validator and (defense-in-depth) in validate_canonical_process_approval_record, and rejects a directly-constructed GeometryAuthorityReference that claims a verified state it did not legitimately inherit. The approval module is split under the 500-line file-size ratchet: derive_governed_approval_event_id and CanonicalProcessApprovalError move to canonical_geometry_process_approval_event.py and are re-exported from the approval module (import identity preserved), so the router and test importers are unaffected.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: PR-1 deliberately deferred the authorization anchor and stamped every approval unverified_pending_governance as a fail-safe; nothing before this makes the verified path reachable, and nothing before this provenance-locks the verified state against direct construction / rehydration. The module split is required because the anchor plus hardening pushed canonical_geometry_process_approval.py past the 500-line ratchet (502 at the anchor commit, higher with the hardening); the split holds the ratchet green without raising the threshold.

commands_run: import identity check -> approval module re-exports CanonicalProcessApprovalError and derive_governed_approval_event_id (is-identity preserved)

files.intent: Add VERIFIED_GOVERNED_PROCESS to ALLOWED_AUTHENTICATION_STATES; add the fail-closed AUTHORIZED_CANONICAL_APPROVERS anchor and is_authorized_canonical_approver() (id+role, fail-closed, system-actor-safe).
files.behavior_change: additive; anchor ships empty so no verified state is minted

files.intent: Decide authentication once via the anchor; provenance-lock the verified state in the model validator and policy gate; slim the module under the 500-line ratchet by re-exporting the extracted event/error symbols.

Extracted sibling module: server-side governed-event derivation (derive_governed_approval_event_id) and CanonicalProcessApprovalError, re-exported from the approval module. Splits the oversized file to hold the file-size ratchet green.
files.behavior_change: code move only; import identity preserved via re-export

files.intent: Centralize the authentication guard on ALLOWED_AUTHENTICATION_STATES and reject a directly-constructed reference claiming a verified state it did not legitimately inherit; split the reference factories out to hold the file-size ratchet (re-exported at the module bottom).
files.behavior_change: additive guard; inherits authentication from the record; factory re-export keeps public API stable

files.intent: Extracted reference-construction factories (create_*_geometry_reference), re-exported from geometry_authority_reference. Splits the oversized reference module under the 500-line ratchet; the model is imported lazily inside the constructing factories so the re-export cycle is import-safe in any order.
files.behavior_change: code move only; import identity preserved via re-export

files.intent: Centralize the canonical-process-authority guard on ALLOWED_AUTHENTICATION_STATES; both permitted states validate green (baseline intentionally not warned to preserve ratified green tests).

Authorization + provenance-lock coverage: authorized->verified, unauthorized->unverified/green, system rejected, forged verified record rejected, forged reference rejected, verified round-trip happy path.
Per-PR CBSP21 manifest for this PR (#202) under the #184 per-PR manifest-discovery model.
